description: Platform-neutral wrapper examples for chat status rendering. These are deterministic examples, not platform SDK fixtures.

expected_response.headline: I need one clarification before routing this.
expected_response.body: The request is not specific enough for a safe workflow handoff.

expected_response.headline: I drafted a plan for this request.
expected_response.body: The user can accept or revise the plan before any handoff is prepared.

scenarios.claim_boundary: A draft plan is not execution evidence.

expected_response.headline: A Codex handoff is ready.
expected_response.body: The handoff is prepared, but executor dispatch is not observed yet.

scenarios.claim_boundary: Prepared handoff is not execution evidence.

expected_response.headline: The handoff was dispatched.
expected_response.body: Executor completion is not observed yet.

expected_response.headline: Executor completion needs review evidence.
expected_response.body: Review evidence is required before completion is reported.

scenarios.claim_boundary: Execution is observed; review is not.

expected_response.headline: Review passed; CI evidence is still missing.
expected_response.body: Merge readiness is not reported until CI evidence is observed.

expected_response.headline: CI is blocking completion.
expected_response.body: The failing or blocked CI checks are surfaced instead of claiming merge readiness.

expected_response.headline: This is ready to merge.
expected_response.body: Execution, verification, review, CI, and merge-readiness evidence are observed.

scenarios.claim_boundary: Ready to merge is not the same as merged.

expected_response.headline: This has been merged.
expected_response.body: Merge evidence is observed in the runtime ledger.

scenarios.claim_boundary: Merged status is backed by runtime ledger evidence.

expected_response.headline: Review passed; CI evidence is still missing.
expected_response.body: A later merge-ready artifact cannot override the missing CI gate.

scenarios.claim_boundary: The upstream CI blocker is shown instead of merge-ready.
